what_happened: Alpha tried to dispatch a test message to itself via skynet_dispatch.py, creating an infinite wait loop that blocked the worker.
root_cause: No guard against self-dispatch in the dispatch pipeline.
fix_applied: Self-dispatch guard added to skynet_dispatch.py -- dispatch_to_worker() now rejects tasks where sender == target.
rule_created: Workers must NEVER dispatch to themselves.

what_happened: Workers instructed to clean up duplicate processes ran Stop-Process and killed the watchdog, SSE daemon, and GOD Console, causing cascading monitoring blindness.
root_cause: Workers had unrestricted process termination privileges. Task instructions were too broad.
fix_applied: Ceasefire directive issued. Services restarted. Kill-prevention rule added. guard_process_kill() added to skynet_dispatch.py.
rule_created: NO worker may terminate any process. Only orchestrator authorized to manage process lifecycle.

what_happened: Multiple watchdogs (4), SSE daemons (4), and GOD Consoles (2) running simultaneously, consuming resources and producing conflicting state.
root_cause: Daemons did not check if another instance was already running before starting.
fix_applied: PID file locking added to skynet_watchdog.py -- checks data/watchdog.pid, verifies process is alive AND is actually a watchdog before exiting.
rule_created: All daemons must implement PID file locking and singleton enforcement before starting.

title: Permission Guard PostMessage Failure
what_happened: guard_permissions() in skynet_start.py used PostMessage WM_KEYDOWN to Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND to select Bypass Approvals from a dropdown. This NEVER worked because VS Code dropdown overlays are Electron webview elements that do NOT receive PostMessage keystrokes when the window lacks OS focus. new_chat.ps1 appeared to work because it runs while the freshly-opened window still has OS focus. UIA reported false success (said 'Bypass') while OCR/screenshots proved the actual screen showed 'Default Approvals'.
root_cause: PostMessage ghost keyboard only works for text input into the main webview content area. It does NOT work for UI control dropdowns, menus, or overlays. UIA status reads can report stale or incorrect state, giving false confidence that the operation succeeded.
fix_applied: SetForegroundWindow before ExpandCollapse, real SendKeys for Enter, restore orchestrator focus after. Visual verification (OCR/screenshots) must be used instead of trusting UIA alone.
rule_created: PostMessage ghost keyboard is ONLY valid for text input into the main webview. For UI control dropdowns, menus, and overlays, use SetForegroundWindow + real SendKeys + restore focus. Always verify UI changes with visual evidence (OCR/screenshots), never trust UIA alone.

root_cause: Service process exited or became unresponsive

new_pid.title: VS Code Quickpick Unreachable via Win32 APIs
new_pid.what_happened: During skynet-start boot, the model guard in new_chat.ps1 could not type into VS Code quickpick dropdown because ALL Win32 input methods (PostMessage, SendInput, keybd_event, clipboard paste, ghost-type, AttachThreadInput) fail to reach Chromium-rendered overlays.
new_pid.fix: pyautogui (real hardware mouse/keyboard) is the ONLY method that works.
new_pid.rule: For VS Code quickpick/dropdown interactions, always use pyautogui, never Win32 APIs.

title: Pre-Fire Visual Proof Downgrade (Rule 0.015)
what_happened: 5 of 10 dispatch/prompt functions lacked screenshot verification before firing into worker/orchestrator windows. Functions clear_steering_and_send(), blast_all(), prompt_worker(), guard_model(), guard_permissions() all executed without visual proof of target state.
root_cause: Commit 11ddba1 (Level 3 truth audit) added pre_dispatch_visual_check() to dispatch_to_worker() ONLY. The same developer did not propagate visual checks to clear_steering_and_send, blast_all, or skynet_start.py functions. No systematic audit of Rule 0.015 compliance was performed.
fix_applied: Dispatched to alpha (skynet_dispatch.py: clear_steering_and_send + blast_all) and beta (skynet_start.py: prompt_worker + guard_model + guard_permissions) to add pre_dispatch_visual_check() gates.
rule_created: Systematic Rule 0.015 audit required after any commit that adds or modifies fire-and-forget functions.
lessons_learned: Rule 0.015 was added for dispatch_to_worker only; 7/10 dispatch functions were never audited
COM threading bug: DXGICapture requires CoInitialize per-thread; multi-threaded UIA scans crashed
Systematic audit of ALL fire-and-forget functions required after any rule addition
Pre-fire screenshot gate is critical for shared-window environments (multi-pane VS Code)
Visual proof prevents orchestrator/consultant misfires in shared windows

title: Ghost-Type NO_EDIT Total Dispatch Failure
what: ALL ghost_type_to_worker() dispatches returned NO_EDIT. Workers showed IDLE forever because prompts never arrived.
root_cause: VS Code Copilot CLI chat input lives inside Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND, not a UIA Edit control. The only Edit control is a 1px accessibility placeholder rejected by the height filter (Height < 10). No fallback existed.
fix: Added Chrome render widget fallback. When no Edit found, focuses Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND directly and pastes. Added _verify_delivery() post-dispatch UIA polling. Commit 806760f.
rule: Ghost-type MUST have fallback delivery via Chrome render widget. Post-dispatch delivery verification via _verify_delivery() is MANDATORY.
lessons_learned: VS Code Copilot CLI chat input lives inside Chrome_RenderWidgetHostHWND, NOT a UIA Edit control
The only Edit control is a 1px accessibility placeholder rejected by height filter (Height < 10)
Ghost-type MUST have Chrome render widget fallback delivery path
Post-dispatch delivery verification via _verify_delivery() prevents silent dispatch failures
UIA Edit-based assumptions break on Electron/Chromium-based chat interfaces

title: Consultant Bridge Queue Had No Consumer - HTTP 202 False Positive
what_happened: The consultant bridge HTTP queue (skynet_consultant_bridge.py on ports 8422/8425) accepted prompt POSTs with HTTP 202 Accepted, creating the illusion of successful delivery. However, NO consumer daemon was running to drain the queue and deliver prompts to the actual consultant VS Code windows. 19 prompts accumulated in the Codex bridge queue with zero delivery. The orchestrator believed consultants were receiving and working on prompts when in reality nothing was being delivered.
root_cause: skynet_consultant_bridge.py was designed as a producer-only HTTP server. It queued incoming prompts to an in-memory list and returned HTTP 202. The design assumed a separate consumer process would poll the queue and deliver prompts via ghost_type to the consultant VS Code window. This consumer was never built. HTTP 202 (Accepted) was interpreted by callers as delivery success when it only meant queued.
fix: Started consultant consumer daemons (PIDs 20972 for Codex, 78528 for Gemini) that poll the bridge queue and deliver via ghost_type_to_worker(). Drained 19 pending prompts from Codex queue. Updated delivery.py to use ghost_type as primary delivery mechanism with bridge as fallback only.
rule_created: HTTP 202 from a queue endpoint does NOT mean delivered. All queue-based systems MUST have a verified consumer. Consultant delivery must use ghost_type primary with bridge fallback.
files_affected: tools/skynet_delivery.py (deliver_to_consultant used HTTP queue as primary)
lessons_learned: HTTP 202 Accepted means queued, NOT delivered - never conflate the two
Every producer needs a verified consumer; untested queue paths create false positives
Consultant bridge was producer-only for the entire system lifetime until discovered
19 prompts silently accumulated without delivery, wasting orchestrator context and time
Always verify end-to-end delivery, not just the first hop (HTTP POST success)

title: Self-Awareness Protocol Violation: Consultant Delivery
what: System-wide Self-Awareness Protocol violation. Nobody analyzed that consultants are VS Code windows identical to workers. Bridge queue approach built without understanding the system.
root_cause: CC/GC-Start never register HWND. State files hardcoded requires_hwnd=false. delivery.py reported success=True for HTTP 202 queued (false positive). No agent traced actual consultant delivery code paths.
fix: CC/GC-Start register HWND via GetForegroundWindow. dispatch uses ghost_type primary. delivery uses ghost_type primary with bridge fallback. consultant_registry.json created.
rule: ALL system participants must understand HOW they deliver/receive prompts. Consultants are VS Code windows. Self-awareness is mandatory.
files_affected: tools/skynet_delivery.py (deliver_to_consultant used HTTP queue, not ghost_type)
lessons_learned: Dual identity systems (worker HWND-based vs consultant HTTP-based) never cross-referenced
Consultants ARE VS Code windows with HWNDs - they receive prompts via ghost_type just like workers
HTTP bridge queue had NO consumer daemon - HTTP 202 was a false positive
requires_hwnd=false in state files was set without understanding that consultants DO have HWNDs
Self-awareness must include ALL system entities, not just the subset you designed first
Consciousness kernel (skynet_self.py) must track consultants alongside workers
9 critical gaps identified in self-awareness architecture gap analysis

title: skynet_arch_verify.py workers.json Type Mismatch Bug
what_happened: Cross-validation round CV1 discovered that tools/skynet_arch_verify.py and tools/skynet_self.py validate_agent_completeness() both assumed data/workers.json was a flat JSON array of worker dicts. In reality, workers.json is a JSON object (dict) with a top-level 'workers' key containing the array. Code like 'for w in workers: w.get(name)' failed with AttributeError because the top-level keys are strings, not dicts.
root_cause: Sprint 2 implementation did not read workers.json before coding the parser. The file format was assumed to be a simple list [{name:alpha,...}] when it is actually {workers:[...], layout:{...}, ...}. Additionally, consultant_state.json uses 'id' not 'name' as the identifier field.
fix: Updated validate_agent_completeness() in skynet_self.py to handle both dict-with-workers-key and list formats. Updated consultant required fields from [name, bridge_port, transport] to [id, transport, role]. Both fixes verified via py_compile and smoke test (validate now returns ALL PASS).
rule_created: Always read data files before writing code that parses them. Never assume JSON structure.
files_affected: data/workers.json (format: dict with workers key, not flat array)
lessons_learned: ALWAYS read the actual data file before writing parsing code
workers.json is a dict with keys: created, engines, god_console_port, monitor, skynet_port, workers, layout
consultant_state.json uses 'id' field not 'name' field for identity
Cross-validation by a different worker (Alpha) caught what implementer (Delta) missed
Bug filed by Alpha during CV1, fixed by Delta - positive-sum scoring in action
